content_en:
Musailamah and Al-Mahkam Ibn At-Tufail were filled with horror. The killing of Ar-Rajjaal spread in Musailamah's army like a fire on a stormy day.

Musailamah used to promise them inevitable victory and that he, Ar-Rajjaal, and Al-Mahkam Ibn Attufail would promulgate their new religion and establish their state on the day following their victory! Now that Ar-Rajjaal was killed, the whole prophecy of Musailamah was seen as a lie, and tomorrow Al-Mahkam and

Musailamah would meet the same fate. Thus the fatal blow of Zaid Ibn Al-Khattaab caused all that destruction in the lines of

No sooner did the Muslims hear the news than they were filled with pride and dignity. The wounded men rose again holding their swords, taking no interest in their wounds.

Even for those who were about to die, nothing connected them

with life except that very faint light caused by hearing the good news which was like avery beautiful and rosy dream. They wished, if

they had any strength to fight with, they would be able to witness the triumph of the battle in its glorious conclusion. But how could that be? Since the doors of Paradise had opened to welcome them, they were now hearing their names while they were being called to immortality!

Zaid raised his hands towards Heaven supplicating Allah and thanking Him for His blessings. Then he returned to his sword and his silence, as he had sworn by Allah not to utter a word until he had completed the victory or gained the honor of martyrdom.

The battle began to lean to the side of the Muslims and their inevitable victory began to approach rapidly. At that moment Zaid did not desire a better conclusion to his life than praying to Allah to grant him martyrdom on that Day of Yamaamah! The wind of Paradise blew to fill his soul with longing, his eyes with tears, and his determination with firmness. He began to fight as if he were searching for his glorious destiny, and the hero fell! So he died a martyr, magnanimously, gracefully and happily. The Army of Islam returned to Al-Madiinah victorious.

While “Umar and the Caliph Abu Bakr were welcoming those who were returning trimuphantly, ‘Umar began to search for his homecoming brother with longing eyes.

Zaid was so tall that he could be easily recognized. But before ‘Umar had strained his eyes, one of the returning Muslims

approached and consoled him. “Umar said, "May Allah have mercy upon Zaid, he preceded me in two instances. He embraced Islam

before me and gained martyrdom before me, too.”

In spite of the victories that Islam won and enjoyed, ‘Umar "AlFaaruugq" (Umar) never forgot his brother Zaid, and he always said, "Whenever the east wind blows, I smell the scent of Zaid."

Yes! The east wind carries the perfume of Zaid (May Allah be pleased with him). But if the caliph gives me permission to add these words tohis great expression so as to complete the meaning of his

saying, these are the words: "Whenever the winds of triumph blow on Islam since the Day of Al-Yamaamah, Islam finds the scent, the

struggles, the heroism, and the greatness of Zaid in these winds!"

Blessings be upon Al-Khattaab under the flag of the Messenger (PBUH). Blessed be they the moment they embraced Islam. Blessed be they when they fought and were martyred. And blessings be upon them in the hereafter.

(27) TALHAH IBN ‘UBAID ALLAH The Falcon on the Day of Uhud !

< Of the believers are men who have been true to their pledge to GOD, from them some have fulfilled their pledges, and some are still in hope of doing so, and they never change at heart > (33 : 23).

The Prophet (PBUH) recited this glorious verse and then turned to his Companions, pointed to Talhah and said, "Anyone who wants to please himself by looking at a man walking on the earth who has fulfilled his pledge of martyrdom should look at Talhah."

The Prophet's Companions never wished nor did their hearts ever aspire and long for a better announcement than the one the Prophet (PBUH) directed to Talhah Ibn “Ubaid Allah. By such words he could feel secure towards his destiny and fate. He was going to live and die as one of those who have been true to their

pledge so that neither civil strife could affect him, nor any kind of lassitude influence him.

The Prophet (PBUH) announced Paradise to him. How then was the life of such a one who deserved this fine announcement?

He was trading in the land of Basraa, when he met one of the most virtuous monks there. He told him that a Prophet who was going to appear in the Sacred Land and whose appearance was prophesied by all virtuous prophets had risen and his era had already begun. Talhah was very much afraid to miss the procession of guidance, mercy, and salvation.

When Talhah returned to his homeland Makkah after having spent months in Basraa and traveling around, he found a lot of talk

taking place here and there. Whenever he met someone or a group of Makkah inhabitants they would talk to him about Muhammad the

Trustworthy, about the angel sent down to him, about the mission he was carrying to the Arabs in particular and all people in general.

The first thing he asked about was Abu Bakr. He learned that

Abu Bakr had returned with a caravan and trade not long ago and that he was standing at the side of Muhammad, believing in and defending him.

Talhah said to himself, "Muhammad and Abu Bakr? By Allah, both of them would never join each other and agree upon falsehood.* Muhammad has already reached the age of 40. In all these years we've never heard him speak one single lie. Is it possible that he would now lie about Allah and say, 'He sent me as a prophet and He sent me an angel’? It's something hard to believe.”

He quickened his steps, directing them towards Abu Bakr's house. They did not talk for long because his long aspiration to meet the Messenger of Allah (PBUH) and to swear to him the oath of allegiance was much faster than his heartbeats.

Abu Bakr accompanied him to the Prophet (PBUH) and he soon embraced Islam, joining there and then the blessed ranks!

That is how Talhah became one of the very early converts.

Despite his honorable rank among his clan, his vast wealth, and his successful trade, he had to taste his own portion of the Quraish's persecution. The task of torturing him and Abu Bakr was given to Nawfal Ibn Khuwailid, who was called The Lion of the Quraish. However, their persecution did not last long, as the Quraish soon felt ashamed and began to think about the consequences of their deeds.

* See our book, Then Came Abu Bakr

Talhah emigrated to Al-Madiinah when the Prophet (PBUH) ordered the Muslims to emigrate. After that he experienced all the battles together with the Prophet (PBUH) except the Battle of Badr because the Prophet (PBUH) had sent him and Sa‘iid Ibn Zaid on an assignment outside Al-Madiinah.

When they had fulfilled their task and were on their way back to Al Madiinah, the Prophet (PBUH) and his Companions were returning home after the battle. Talhah and his companion felt so sad

and tormented for having missed the reward of joining the Prophet (PBUH) in his first jihaad battle.

However, the Prophet (PBUH) accorded them peace of mind when he informed them that their reward was exactly like the warrior's reward; moreover, he gave them a share of the booty exactly like the share he gave to each one who had fought the battle.

Then came the Battle of Uhud, when the Quraish, with all their might and tyranny, came to take blood revenge for the Day of Badr and to restore their dignity by defeating the Muslims once and for all, a defeat which was thought by the Quraish to be a simple matter and a predetermined fate. The fierce battle took place, and soon the battlefield was filled with its awful harvest: calamity overtook the polytheists.

Then when the Muslims saw them retreating, they laid down their weapons and the archers descended from their posts and began to collect their share of booty. Immediately and suddenly the Quraish army turned back to hold the field and tip the balance of the battle in their favor.

The fighting's ferocity, cruelty, and crushing resumed. The surprise attack had the effect of scattering the army.

Talhah saw that the side of the battlefield where the Prophet (PBUH) was standing had become the target of the polytheists'

concentration. He immediately hurried towards the Prophet (PBUH).

He (May Allah be pleased with him) traversed a path, a long one, although it was in fact a short distance.

It was a path in which a single inch could not be traversed

except by confronting tens of ferocious swords and tens of mad lances.

He could see from a far distance how the Prophet's (PBUH) cheek was bleeding and how he was silently suffering. It was then that Talhah got mad, leaped once or twice over the path of horror to reach the Prophet (PBUH), in front of whom he had to experience what he was afraid of: the swords of the polytheists drawn towards the Prophet (PBUH), surrounding him, wanting to get at him.

Talhah stood there like a raging army, striking with his sword to the left and right. He could see the Prophet (PBUH) bleeding and his pains becoming more and more unbearable. He helped him and carried him away from the hole where his foot had gotten stuck.

He supported the Prophet (PBUH) with his left hand and chest, backing up to a safe, secure place, while his right hand (May Allah bless his right hand) fought the swords of the polytheists who surrounded the Prophet and who swarmed the battlefield like locusts.

Let Abu Bakr As-Siddiiq describe for us the whole scene of battle. “Aa'ishah once said: Whenever Abu Bakr recalled the Day of Uhud he used to say, It was the Talhah's day. I was the first who approached the Prophet (PBUH). He said to me and to Abu “Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarraah, "Watch out, for your brother." We looked at him, and we could see more than 70 stabs. His finger was cut off. We tried to remedy his condition.

In all the different events and battles, Talhah was always to be found in the forefront fighting in the cause of Allah, redeeming the Prophet's standard.

Talhah lived among the Muslim community, worshipping Allah with the worshipers, fighting in the cause of Allah with those who fought for truth, following the basic principles of the new religion which was revealed in order to bring people — all people — out of
content_id:
Musailamah dan Al-Mahkam Ibn Al-Thufail dipenuhi rasa ketakutan. Terbunuhnya Al-Rajjal menyebar di pasukan Musailamah seperti api di hari yang berangin kencang.

Musailamah biasa menjanjikan kemenangan yang tak terelakkan kepada mereka dan bahwa beliau, Al-Rajjal, dan Al-Mahkam Ibn Al-Thufail akan menyebarkan agama baru mereka dan mendirikan negara mereka pada hari setelah kemenangan mereka! Kini Al-Rajjal telah terbunuh, seluruh ramalan Musailamah terlihat sebagai kebohongan, dan esok hari Al-Mahkam dan

Musailamah akan menghadapi nasib yang sama. Maka pukulan mematikan Zaid Ibn Al-Khattab menimbulkan semua kehancuran itu dalam barisan-barisan

Begitu kaum Muslimin mendengar kabar itu, mereka dipenuhi kebanggaan dan kehormatan. Orang-orang yang terluka bangkit kembali sambil memegang pedang mereka, tidak menghiraukan luka-luka mereka.

Bahkan bagi mereka yang hampir meninggal, tidak ada hal yang

menghubungkan mereka dengan kehidupan kecuali cahaya yang sangat redup yang disebabkan oleh mendengar kabar baik yang bagaikan mimpi yang sangat indah dan cerah. Mereka berharap, jika

mereka masih memiliki kekuatan untuk bertempur, mereka akan bisa menyaksikan kemenangan pertempuran dalam kesimpulannya yang mulia. Namun bagaimana hal itu bisa terjadi? Karena pintu-pintu Surga telah terbuka untuk menyambut mereka, mereka kini mendengar nama-nama mereka dipanggil menuju keabadian!

Zaid mengangkat tangannya ke langit memohon kepada Allah dan bersyukur kepada-Nya atas nikmat-Nya. Kemudian beliau kembali kepada pedangnya dan keheningannya, karena beliau telah bersumpah demi Allah untuk tidak mengucapkan sepatah kata pun hingga beliau menyelesaikan kemenangan atau mendapatkan kehormatan syahadah.

Pertempuran mulai condong ke sisi kaum Muslimin dan kemenangan mereka yang tak terelakkan mulai mendekat dengan cepat. Pada saat itu Zaid tidak menginginkan kesimpulan yang lebih baik bagi hidupnya selain berdoa kepada Allah untuk menganugerahkan syahadah kepadanya pada Hari Al-Yamamah! Semilir angin Surga berhembus mengisi jiwanya dengan kerinduan, matanya dengan air mata, dan tekadnya dengan keteguhan. Beliau mulai bertempur seolah sedang mencari takdirnya yang mulia, dan sang pahlawan pun gugur! Maka beliau wafat sebagai syahid, dengan jiwa besar, anggun, dan bahagia. Pasukan Islam kembali ke Al-Madiinah dalam keadaan menang.

Sementara 'Umar dan Khalifah Abu Bakar menyambut mereka yang kembali dengan kemenangan, 'Umar mulai mencari saudaranya yang pulang dengan mata penuh kerinduan.

Zaid sangat tinggi sehingga bisa dengan mudah dikenali. Namun sebelum 'Umar memaksakan matanya, salah satu dari kaum Muslimin yang kembali

mendekat dan menghiburnya. 'Umar berkata, "Semoga Allah merahmati Zaid, beliau mendahuluiku dalam dua hal. Beliau memeluk Islam

sebelumku dan mendapatkan syahadah sebelumku juga."

Meskipun kemenangan-kemenangan yang diraih dan dinikmati Islam, 'Umar "Al-Faruq" tidak pernah melupakan saudaranya Zaid, dan beliau selalu berkata, "Setiap kali angin timur berhembus, aku mencium aroma Zaid."

Ya! Angin timur membawa harum wewangian Zaid (ra). Namun jika khalifah mengizinkanku untuk menambahkan kata-kata ini pada ungkapannya yang agung untuk melengkapi makna

perkataannya, inilah kata-katanya: "Setiap kali angin kemenangan berhembus atas Islam sejak Hari Al-Yamamah, Islam menemukan aroma, perjuangan, kepahlawanan, dan kebesaran Zaid dalam angin-angin ini!"

Semoga keberuntungan dilimpahkan atas Al-Khattab di bawah panji Rasulullah ﷺ. Semoga diberkahi saat mereka memeluk Islam. Semoga diberkahi ketika mereka bertempur dan meraih syahadah. Dan semoga keberuntungan dilimpahkan atas mereka di akhirat.

(27) THALHAH IBN 'UBAID ALLAH Elang pada Hari Uhud!

< Di antara kaum beriman ada orang-orang yang menepati apa yang telah mereka janjikan kepada Allah, di antara mereka ada yang sudah menepatinya dan di antara mereka ada pula yang menunggu-nunggu dan mereka tidak merubah sedikit pun > (33:23).

Nabi ﷺ membacakan ayat mulia ini kemudian berpaling kepada para sahabatnya, menunjuk kepada Thalhah dan bersabda, "Siapa yang ingin menyenangkan dirinya dengan melihat seorang laki-laki yang berjalan di bumi yang telah menepati janji syahadahnya hendaklah melihat kepada Thalhah."

Para sahabat Nabi ﷺ tidak pernah mengharapkan dan hati mereka tidak pernah merindukan pengumuman yang lebih baik dari yang disampaikan Nabi ﷺ kepada Thalhah Ibn 'Ubaid Allah. Dengan kata-kata seperti itu beliau bisa merasa aman terhadap takdir dan nasibnya. Beliau akan hidup dan mati sebagai salah satu dari mereka yang telah menepati janji mereka sehingga tidak ada fitnah yang bisa mempengaruhinya, tidak ada kelemahan apa pun yang bisa memengaruhinya.

Nabi ﷺ mengumumkan Surga untuknya. Lalu bagaimanakah kehidupan orang yang berhak mendapatkan pengumuman mulia itu?

Beliau sedang berdagang di negeri Basrah, ketika beliau bertemu salah satu rahib yang paling saleh di sana. Rahib itu memberitahunya bahwa seorang Nabi yang akan muncul di Tanah Suci dan yang kemunculannya telah diramalkan oleh semua nabi yang saleh telah bangkit dan eranya sudah dimulai. Thalhah sangat takut untuk melewatkan rombongan petunjuk, rahmat, dan keselamatan.

Ketika Thalhah kembali ke tanah airnya Makkah setelah menghabiskan berbulan-bulan di Basrah dan berkeliling, beliau menemukan banyak pembicaraan di mana-mana. Setiap kali beliau bertemu seseorang atau sekelompok penduduk Makkah, mereka berbicara tentang Muhammad

yang Terpercaya, tentang malaikat yang diturunkan kepadanya, tentang misi yang dibawanya kepada orang-orang Arab pada khususnya dan semua orang pada umumnya.

Hal pertama yang ditanyakannya adalah tentang Abu Bakar. Beliau mengetahui bahwa

Abu Bakar baru saja kembali dengan kafilah dan perdagangan tidak lama sebelumnya dan bahwa beliau berdiri di sisi Muhammad, beriman dan membelanya.

Thalhah berkata dalam hati, "Muhammad dan Abu Bakar? Demi Allah, keduanya tidak akan pernah bersatu dan menyepakati kebatilan. Muhammad telah berusia 40 tahun. Selama semua tahun ini kami tidak pernah mendengarnya berbicara satu kebohongan pun. Apakah mungkin beliau sekarang berbohong tentang Allah dan berkata, 'Dia mengutusnya sebagai nabi dan mengirim malaikat kepadanya'? Itu sesuatu yang sulit dipercaya."

Beliau mempercepat langkahnya, mengarahkannya ke rumah Abu Bakar. Mereka tidak berbincang lama karena hasrat panjangnya untuk bertemu Rasulullah ﷺ dan berbaiat kepadanya jauh lebih cepat dari detak jantungnya.

Abu Bakar menemaninya kepada Nabi ﷺ dan beliau segera memeluk Islam, bergabung saat itu juga ke barisan yang diberkahi!

Demikianlah Thalhah menjadi salah satu dari mereka yang paling awal memeluk Islam.

Meskipun kedudukannya yang terhormat di antara klannya, kekayaannya yang besar, dan perdagangannya yang sukses, beliau harus merasakan bagiannya dari penganiayaan Quraisy. Tugas menyiksa beliau dan Abu Bakar diberikan kepada Nawfal Ibn Khuwailid, yang disebut Singa Quraisy. Namun penganiayaan mereka tidak berlangsung lama, karena Quraisy segera merasa malu dan mulai memikirkan konsekuensi perbuatan mereka.

Thalhah berhijrah ke Al-Madiinah ketika Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan kaum Muslimin untuk berhijrah. Setelah itu beliau mengalami semua pertempuran bersama Nabi ﷺ kecuali Perang Badr karena Nabi ﷺ telah mengutus beliau dan Sa'id Ibn Zaid dalam sebuah tugas di luar Al-Madiinah.

Ketika mereka telah menyelesaikan tugas mereka dan dalam perjalanan kembali ke Al-Madiinah, Nabi ﷺ dan para sahabatnya sedang pulang setelah pertempuran. Thalhah dan sahabatnya merasa sangat sedih

dan tersiksa karena melewatkan pahala bergabung dengan Nabi ﷺ dalam pertempuran jihad pertamanya.

Namun Nabi ﷺ memberi mereka ketenangan pikiran ketika memberitahu mereka bahwa pahala mereka persis sama dengan pahala para pejuang; terlebih lagi, beliau memberikan mereka bagian dari rampasan perang persis sama seperti bagian yang diberikan kepada setiap orang yang telah bertempur dalam pertempuran itu.

Kemudian datanglah Perang Uhud, ketika Quraisy dengan segala kekuatan dan kekejamannya datang untuk membalas dendam atas Hari Badr dan memulihkan martabat mereka dengan mengalahkan kaum Muslimin sekali untuk selamanya, sebuah kekalahan yang dianggap oleh Quraisy sebagai hal yang mudah dan takdir yang telah ditentukan. Pertempuran sengit pun terjadi, dan segera medan perang dipenuhi panen mengerikannya: bencana menimpa kaum musyrik.

Kemudian ketika kaum Muslimin melihat mereka mundur, mereka meletakkan senjata mereka dan para pemanah turun dari pos-pos mereka dan mulai mengumpulkan bagian rampasan perang mereka. Segera dan tiba-tiba pasukan Quraisy berbalik untuk menguasai medan dan membalik keseimbangan pertempuran demi kepentingan mereka.

Keganasan, kekejaman, dan kekerasan pertempuran kembali berlanjut. Serangan mendadak itu berdampak menceraiberaikan pasukan.

Thalhah melihat bahwa sisi medan pertempuran tempat Nabi ﷺ berdiri telah menjadi target konsentrasi kaum musyrik.

Beliau segera bergegas menuju Nabi ﷺ.

Beliau (ra) menempuh sebuah jalan, yang panjang, meskipun sebenarnya jarak yang pendek.

Itu adalah jalan yang bahkan satu inci pun tidak bisa ditempuh

kecuali dengan menghadapi puluhan pedang yang ganas dan puluhan tombak yang liar.

Beliau dapat melihat dari jarak jauh betapa pipi Nabi ﷺ berdarah dan betapa beliau ﷺ menderita dalam diam. Saat itulah Thalhah menjadi gila, melompat sekali atau dua kali melewati jalan kengerian untuk mencapai Nabi ﷺ, di hadapannya beliau harus menghadapi apa yang ditakutinya: pedang-pedang kaum musyrik yang terhunus menuju Nabi ﷺ, mengelilinginya, ingin mendekat.

Thalhah berdiri di sana seperti pasukan yang mengamuk, menebas dengan pedangnya ke kiri dan kanan. Beliau dapat melihat Nabi ﷺ berdarah dan rasa sakitnya semakin tak tertahankan. Beliau membantunya dan membawanya pergi dari lubang tempat kakinya tersangkut.

Beliau menopang Nabi ﷺ dengan tangan kiri dan dadanya, mundur ke tempat yang aman dan terlindung, sementara tangan kanannya (semoga Allah memberkati tangan kanannya) memerangi pedang-pedang kaum musyrik yang mengelilingi Nabi ﷺ dan yang memenuhi medan perang seperti belalang.

Biarkan Abu Bakar al-Siddiq menggambarkan kepada kita seluruh adegan pertempuran. 'Aisyah pernah berkata: Setiap kali Abu Bakar mengenang Hari Uhud beliau biasa berkata, Itu adalah hari Thalhah. Aku adalah yang pertama mendekati Nabi ﷺ. Beliau berkata kepadaku dan kepada Abu 'Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarrah, "Perhatikanlah saudaramu." Kami memandangnya, dan kami dapat melihat lebih dari 70 luka tikam. Jarinya terpotong. Kami berusaha mengobati kondisinya.

Dalam semua kejadian dan pertempuran yang berbeda, Thalhah selalu berada di garis terdepan bertempur di jalan Allah, menebus panji Nabi ﷺ.

Thalhah hidup di antara komunitas Muslim, beribadah kepada Allah bersama para ahli ibadah, berperang di jalan Allah bersama mereka yang berjuang untuk kebenaran, mengikuti prinsip-prinsip dasar agama baru yang diwahyukan untuk membawa manusia — semua manusia — keluar dari
